/answer>From the options given, I will now determine the most typical action for a right-handed person in the described scenario. The task involves flinging a palm-sized object off a table surface using my right hand. As a right-handed individual, I would typically have my arm in an extended position, with my hand raised above head height. This initial hand height allows me to generate enough force to propel the object away from the table. Given the size of the object and the need for distance, I would apply a moderate force to ensure it flies effectively. The object needs to be moved forward, so the horizontal direction should be forward. For the vertical direction, I would release the object downward at the peak of its extension to maximize its forward motion. Therefore, the action can be described as: "With my arm extended and my hand held high above head height, I apply a moderate force to fling the palm-sized object forward in a downward direction."
